name: QuarterTurn to Right
starting_position: Closed Position or Outside Partner Position (depending on previous figure)
ending_position: Square, inline, step in between man's legs
preceding_figure_names: Closed Telemark, Fish Tail, Four Quick Run, Progressive Chasse, Running Right Turn, V6
following_figure_names: Progressive Chasse, Reverse Pivot, Heel Pivot (Quarter Turn To Left), Four Quick Run, V6 (2-12, L heel should still lower on preceeding step), Six Quick Run (L heel should still lower on preceeding step), Hover Corte
notes:
If this figure starts facing LOD:
Leader: turn 1/4 to R between 1-2, and 1/8 between 2-3.
Follower: turn 3/8 to R between 1-2, body turns less, finish the turn on 3.
Don't let head and shoulder weight drop back. It's controlled action.

steps.action: RF forward, FDW, HT, start to turn R, sway S, CBM, start to rise e/o 1
comments: Roll your foot, so it's not jerky.

steps.action: LF side, BC, T, 1/8 to R between 1-2, sway R, cont to rise on 2 and 3
RF closes to LF, BDC, T, 1/8 to R between 2-3, sway R, No rise & fall
LF side/slightly back, BDC, TH, --, sway S, Up, lower e/o 4
comments: Don't let head and shoulder weight drop back. It's controlled action.

steps.action: LF back, BDW, TH, start to turn R, sway S, CBM, start to rise e/o 1, NFR
comments: When starting a dance, this is not OP. After initial figure, this will always be OP.

steps.action: RF side, PDC, T, 1/4 to R between 1-2, body turns less, sway L, cont to rise on 2 and 3
comments: Keep head to left, do not allow head and shoulder to start going to the right. In Quarter Turn, the turn happens sooner (compared to heel turns), swing R leg/hip to side. Compared to Natural Turn, this is less effort (less turn).

steps.action: LF close to RF, FDC, T, slight body turn, sway L, --, No rise & fall
comments: In Quarter Turn, there is a check point at end of step 3 (not like Chasse which progressive, trapezoid movement).

steps.action: RF diagonal fwd, FDC, TH, --, sway S, Up, lower e/o 4
comments: Finish square, inline. Step in between Man's legs.
